name: Mexican Shredded Spiced Beef (Carnitas)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300 degrees F. Trim excess fat from roast.
2. Mix together salsa, garlic, chili powder, oregano, and cumin to make a paste.
Spread the paste evenly on the roast.
3. Wrap the roast tightly in a large piece of aluminum foil, folding the edges
to seal completely. Place the wrapped roast in a roasting pan with the seam
of the foil facing up.
4. Bake until the meat is extremely tender and can be shredded easily,
approximately 4 to 4-1/2 hours.
5. Remove the meat from the oven; discard the fat and bones. Skim any excess
fat from the drippings and reserve them. Once the roast is cool enough to handle,
shred the meat.
6. Transfer the shredded meat and drippings to a large pan. Stir in the
canned tomatoes and their juice, breaking them into small pieces. Cook until
heated through, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and adjust flavors as needed,
adding more salsa or seasonings if desired.
Makes about 7 cups shredded beef (enough for approximately twenty 8-inch tortillas).
Timesaver Tip: Beef can be prepared up to 3 days in advance and stored
in the refrigerator. It can also be frozen at 0 degrees F for up to 3 months.
Package the beef in meal-sized servings before freezing.
